Value Creation Thesis

Modeled $3.0M recoverable gross profit from controllable drivers already visible in the current business: concentrated in pricing discipline, close-rate gap, and warranty leakage. Before acquisitions or incremental headcount; EBITDA flow-through ≈ 70-85% of recoverable GP at fixed overhead.

12-mo GP Unlock
$3.0M
across 5 controllable drivers

30 / 60 / 90 Operating Plan

How to run the week from the dashboard

Three windows, each grounded in the metrics above. Each window locks in the prior window's gains.

Days 0 – 30 Establish the baseline

Objective Establish the baseline.
Primary drivers Pricing variance, estimate conversion, warranty & callback leakage.
weekly rhythm Weekly operating review: same six tiles every Monday.
Expected output Signed-off baseline P&L by market × trade | first manager scorecards.

Days 31 – 60 Install accountability

Objective Install accountability.
Primary drivers Utilization Proxy, close rate, membership attach.
weekly rhythm Branch & trade scorecards | pricebook discount authority >10% locked to dispatch.
Expected output First round of manager action plans for bottom-quartile close rate & utilization.

Days 61 – 90 Scale what works

Objective Scale what works.
Primary drivers Market playbooks (Bay Area HVAC → Out-of-Area), margin review, board packet.
weekly rhythm Monthly P&L variance review.
Expected output Repeatable management operating system: daily / weekly / monthly cadences fixed.

Revenue Basis
ServiceTitan completed-job revenue only. Reported company revenue is typically higher because this basis excludes: accrual / GAAP adjustments, non-ServiceTitan revenue, commercial-line revenue, financing income, rebates, warranty-accounting, and any AR not closed at the job level. The $44.6M LTM revenue shown here should be read as the operational-cash-revenue floor, not the audited top line.
GP Unlock vs EBITDA
The headline $3.0M is a modeled recoverable gross profit from controllable drivers already visible in the current business: before acquisitions or incremental headcount. Estimated EBITDA flow-through ≈ 70-85% of recoverable GP assuming overhead stays largely fixed at execution scale (~$2.1-2.6M EBITDA-adj). Each driver's confidence is shown inline.
Measured vs Modeled
Measured from export: revenue, GM, ticket, completed jobs, callbacks, membership flag. Estimated: Open Estimate Pipeline (count × avg ticket × 0.62 × 0.97), Backlog Revenue at Risk. Modeled: Technician Add-On Conversion, EBITDA flow-through, Capacity scenarios, Net Membership Growth. Proxy: Tech Utilization (paid_time includes PTO/training, not yet normalized).

Coefficients data-derived from trailing 12mo (2025-05-01 to 2026-04-30): 1pp windshield ≈ 1.19% capacity | 1pp zero-ticket ≈ 1.20% revenue | 1pp flip ≈ 0.85% revenue. Baselines: $48.2M annual rev | 195,838 paid hours | 98 implied techs.

RoadmapValue Creation Roadmap EBITDA bridge + driver decomposition | ServiceTitan-grounded

Operating diligence view: every driver quantified from the ServiceTitan job log. Each card answers a specific question the deal partner asked. The bridge at the top sums to a defensible organic EBITDA target: no acquisitions, no synthetic deltas, no unverified assumptions.

EBITDA Bridge: 2025 baseline → operator target

Bay Area vs Out-of-Area: operator scorecard (2025)

Techs assigned to primary market by majority of completed jobs

Close-rate gap by trade: bottom-quartile lift to median

Per-tech 2025 conversion (Converted / Completed)

True tech utilization: Hours Worked / Paid Time

2025 | 30+ jobs | 200+ paid hours

Pricing decomposition

Per-tech price-variance (vs pricebook) + member ticket spread by trade

Price-variance distribution (per tech, %)

Member vs non-member ticket: by market × trade

Membership economics: recurring revenue base

Member-attached penetration, retention proxy, per-tech variation

Member penetration by trade (2025)

Top member-attached techs (2025)

Callback / true-cancel compression

2025 cancels (10-day reschedule rule): target as the target

Data hygiene: campaign subcategory mapping

Backfill rules applied for "(unspecified)" patterns
All driver lifts use 2025 ServiceTitan as baseline | Blended GM% applied to revenue lift to estimate EBITDA delta | No synthetic prior-period values | Operator decisions on Out-of-Area, M&A roadmap, and tech-by-name PIPs require human judgment beyond the dashboard.
